entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: Country where rainfall variability and Vertisol management are studied

entities.name: RAINFALL
entities.description: Atmospheric water falling in India, variable in intensity, duration, and distribution

entities.name: RAINFALL EXCESS
entities.description: Runoff resulting from high-intensity storms, especially in low-infiltration soils

entities.name: VERTISOLS
entities.description: Type of soil characterized by low infiltration rates

entities.name: INFILTRATION RATES
entities.description: The rate at which water enters the soil, which is low in Vertisols

entities.name: SOIL EROSION
entities.description: The process of soil degradation caused by runoff

entities.name: CROP PRODUCTION POTENTIAL
entities.description: The capacity of the soil resource to support crop production

entities.name: GRADED BROADBEDS AND FURROWS
entities.description: A system developed at ICRISAT to improve surface drainage and collect runoff

entities.name: SURFACE DRAINAGE
entities.description: The movement of water across the soil surface

entities.name: MONSOON SEASON
entities.description: The seasonal period characterized by high rainfall

entities.name: RAINY SEASON CROPPING
entities.description: The practice of growing crops during the rainy season

entities.name: STORAGE FACILITIES
entities.description: Facilities used to collect and store runoff water for later use

entities.name: STRESS PERIODS
entities.description: Periods during the crop cycle where water is scarce
